pi_items.source: Verified via POE5 (Principles of Econometrics 5th Edition) documentation. The truffles dataset is a standard teaching dataset included in the PoEdata package for R and available in multiple formats (CSV, Gretl, etc.) at http://www.principlesofeconometrics.com/poe5/
Verified via setup_task.sh (line 14) and export_result.sh (line 14) which both define OUTPUT_PATH variable pointing to this exact path

Verified via multiple sources: (1) Wikipedia article on Simultaneous Equations Model (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Simultaneous_equations_model) states 3SLS is 'a method of estimation that combines system equation with two-stage least squares'; (2) Stata documentation (https://www.stata.com/manuals13/rreg3.pdf) confirms 3SLS for systems of simultaneous equations; (3) Purdue University econometrics materials (https://engineering.purdue.edu/~flm/CE615_files/3SLS-lecture.pdf) confirm 3SLS is appropriate for simultaneous equations with endogenous variables

Verified via economic theory: (1) Wikipedia Price Elasticity of Demand (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Price_elasticity_of_demand) states 'price elasticity of demand is ordinarily negative because quantity demanded falls when price rises'; (2) Khan Academy (https://www.khanacademy.org/economics-finance-domain/microeconomics/supply-demand-equilibrium/supply-curve-tutorial/a/law-of-supply) documents Law of Demand; (3) Multiple econometrics sources confirm demand equation: Q_d = f(P, ...) has ∂Q_d/∂P < 0, resulting in negative coefficient on P

Verified via economic theory: (1) Wikipedia Law of Supply (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Law_of_supply) states 'increase in price results in an increase in quantity supplied'; (2) Khan Academy and multiple econometrics sources confirm Law of Supply shows positive relationship between price and quantity supplied; (3) Supply equation: Q_s = f(P, ...) has ∂Q_s/∂P > 0, resulting in positive coefficient on P

The task's embedded data in setup_task.sh (lines 29-81) is different from official POE5 truffles data: (1) Price ranges: embedded 45.00-60.50 (mean 51.60) vs POE5 29.64-105.45 (mean 62.72); (2) Quantity ranges: embedded 9.10-12.00 (mean 10.45) vs POE5 6.37-26.27 (mean 18.46); (3) Disposable income: embedded 2840-3200 vs POE5 1.52-5.12 (different scale); (4) Zero exact price value matches between datasets. The embedded data appears to be pedagogically simplified synthetic data for the task rather than authentic POE5 data

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the truffles dataset, a standard simultaneous equations teaching dataset from Principles of Econometrics (POE5). The dataset contains 30 observations with variables: quantity (q), price (p), substitute price (ps), disposable income (di), and factor price (pf). The task correctly employs 3SLS (Three-Stage Least Squares) as the estimation method, which is the appropriate econometric technique for simultaneous equation systems with endogenous variables. Economic theory confirms the expected coefficient signs: demand equation should show negative price coefficient (Law of Demand: quantity demanded decreases as price rises) and supply equation should show positive price coefficient (Law of Supply: quantity supplied increases as price rises). The actual data embedded in the task setup script is synthetic/modified pedagogical data that differs from the official POE5 truffles dataset in ranges and distributions.
